seoTitle: Dandaloo vs Dangar Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Dandaloo and Dangar Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Dandaloo vs Dangar Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dandaloo and Dangar Island.
heading: Dandaloo vs Dangar Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dandaloo, New South Wales and Dangar Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dandaloo has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Dangar Island (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dandaloo or Dangar Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dandaloo has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Dangar Island scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dandaloo scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Dangar Island scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dandaloo or Dangar Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dandaloo has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
